complete_raw_email_data_from_gmail_api_i: Complete raw email data from Gmail API (if enabled)
condense_a_long_email_thread_into_the_ke: Condense a long email thread into the key points, decisions, and action items.
describe_the_email_you_want_to_write: Describe the email you want to write...
describe_what_emails_you_want_to_find: Describe what emails you want to find...
draft_a_contextual_reply_to_an_email_in: Draft a contextual reply to an email in the right tone, ready for review.
enter_gmail_label_name_e_g_inbox_sent: Enter Gmail label name (e.g., INBOX, SENT, or custom label)
enter_label_id_to_remove_e_g_inbox: Enter label ID to remove (e.g., INBOX)
gmail_message_id_for_the_draft: Gmail message ID for the draft
gmail_message_id_not_rfc_message_id_use: Gmail message ID (not RFC Message-ID) - use the "id" field from results
gmail_message_id_to_reply_to_use_id: Gmail message ID to reply to (use "id" field from results, not "messageId")
id_of_the_draft_to_update: ID of the draft to update
id_of_the_email_to_move: ID of the email to move
integrate_gmail_into_the_workflow_can_se: Integrate Gmail into the workflow. Can send, read, search, and move emails. Can be used in trigger mode to trigger a workflow when a new email is received.
label: Label
label_id_for_add_remove_operations_canon: Label ID for add/remove operations (canonical param)
message_identifier_for_reading_specific_: Message identifier for reading specific email
read_specific_email_by_id_overrides_labe: Read specific email by ID (overrides label/folder)
save_incoming_emails_to_notion_databases: Save incoming emails to Notion databases
search_gmail_for_messages_matching_a_que: Search Gmail for messages matching a query and extract the details you need.
send_read_search_and_move_gmail_messages: Send, read, search, and move Gmail messages or trigger workflows from Gmail events
sort_unread_email_by_urgency_and_draft_r: Sort unread email by urgency and draft replies for the most important messages.
subject: Subject
thread_id_to_reply_to_for_threading: Thread ID to reply to (for threading)
